gallery.tagline: Interactive protocol visualizations for network engineers, students, and anyone curious about what happens on the wire.
gallery.aboutDescription: ProtoViz is an open-source platform for creating, sharing, and interactively exploring protocol exchange scenarios. Each scenario is a YAML file describing a complete protocol interaction — with every frame field annotated with spec references and Linux kernel source cross-links.
gallery.disclaimer: Protocol descriptions are based on open-source implementations (Linux kernel, rdma-core) and public RFCs. Specification references are citations for further reading — no proprietary text has been reproduced.
gallery.gsLearnDesc: Pick a scenario below, step through frame-by-frame, and follow the guided walkthrough to understand each protocol exchange.
gallery.gsAnalyzeDesc: Upload a PCAP file to inspect packets, get compliance findings, and generate shareable scenarios from your own traffic.
gallery.gsLiveDesc: Send a real message and watch every protocol layer animate as your data travels the network stack.
gallery.gsCreateDesc: Build custom protocol scenarios using the YAML editor, preview them, and share with your team.

protocolFamily.tcpIpDesc: Core internet protocols: TCP handshakes, TLS encryption, ARP/NDP resolution, and what really happens when you click a link.
protocolFamily.ipv6Desc: Next-generation internet protocol: autoconfiguration, dual-stack fallback, DHCPv6, and transition mechanisms like NAT64.
protocolFamily.interconnectDesc: Physical layer concepts: serial vs. parallel signaling, byte order, and forward error correction.
protocolFamily.rdmaDesc: Remote Direct Memory Access: zero-copy data transfers, lossless Ethernet, GPU-to-GPU communication, and AI training collectives.
protocolFamily.nvmeOfDesc: Non-Volatile Memory Express over RDMA and TCP: discovery, connection, and high-performance storage I/O.
protocolFamily.sanDesc: Fibre Channel fabric login, iSCSI sessions, and SCSI command processing over dedicated storage networks.
protocolFamily.storageDesc: GPUDirect Storage: NVMe and NVMe-oF data paths directly to GPU memory, bypassing the CPU.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.intro: ProtoViz is open source and welcomes contributions — new scenarios, compliance rules, protocol dissectors, and code improvements.
contributing.scenarioCreatorDesc: Click "Create Scenario" on the landing page, provide an Anthropic API key, describe the protocol exchange in plain English, and generate a complete YAML scenario.
contributing.fromPcapDesc: Use the Python converter: python tools/converter.py capture.pcap --out public/scenarios/my-protocol/scenario.yaml
contributing.yamlStructureDesc: Each scenario defines: meta (title, protocol, authors, specs), topology (actors and links), osi_layers (per-actor layer state), frames (packet definitions with headers and fields), timeline (ordered protocol events), and glossary (term definitions).
contributing.prRequirementsDesc: Valid YAML conforming to scenario.schema.json. Every field needs a description. Headers should reference spec sections (RFCs, IEEE, IBTA). AI-generated content must be reviewed for accuracy. Include your name in meta.authors.
contributing.rulesAndDissectorsDesc: Compliance rules live in public/rules/ as JSON. Protocol dissectors are JavaScript modules in src/pcap/dissectors/. Both follow existing patterns — check the RoCEv2 implementations as examples.
contributing.codeStyleDesc: React 18 functional components, Zustand for state management, inline styles. Run npm run test before submitting.

viewer.detailPanelSeparate: Detail panel is in a separate window — click to focus

chat.title: CHAT
chat.apiKeyStorage: Stored in localStorage. Sent only to api.anthropic.com.
chat.emptyHint: Ask about the current protocol step, packet fields, spec details, or anything RDMA/networking related.
chat.suggestStep: What is happening at this step?
chat.suggestRkey: What happens if the rkey is wrong?

about.tagline: Interactive protocol education platform for network engineers, students, and anyone curious about what happens on the wire.
about.disclaimer: Protocol descriptions are based on open-source implementations (Linux kernel, rdma-core) and public RFCs. Specification references are citations for further reading — no proprietary text has been reproduced.

troubleshooter.title: PCAP Troubleshooter
troubleshooter.uploadDescription: Upload a PCAP file or tshark JSON export to analyze protocol compliance. All parsing happens locally in your browser — nothing is uploaded to any server.
troubleshooter.pcapDescription: Standard packet capture (.pcap, .pcapng, .cap). Built-in dissectors for Ethernet, IPv4, TCP, UDP, and RoCEv2.
troubleshooter.tsharkDescription: Full protocol dissection via Wireshark. Supports 3000+ protocols.
troubleshooter.sensitiveDataWarning: — Payload bytes containing potential credentials or PII were found. Raw payload content is automatically excluded from AI chat context.
troubleshooter.scrubLabel: Scrub sensitive data (replace real IPs and MACs)
troubleshooter.tcpdumpDetected: This looks like tcpdump text output
troubleshooter.tcpdumpExplanation: tcpdump text output doesn't include the raw packet bytes needed for full protocol analysis. For the best results, save your capture in binary format.

traceChat.title: TRACE CHAT

traceChat.emptyHint: Ask about your trace — protocol issues, packet behavior, troubleshooting advice.
traceChat.suggestWrong: What went wrong in this trace?
traceChat.suggestRst: Why is there a TCP RST?

helloChat.title: Hello World Chat
helloChat.subtitle: Send a real message and watch every protocol layer animate as your data travels the network stack.
helloChat.modeSameMachineDesc: Two browser tabs on this computer. Messages travel through BroadcastChannel — never leave your machine.
helloChat.modeSameNetworkDesc: Two devices on your local WiFi or LAN. Messages travel directly between browsers via WebRTC — true peer-to-peer.
helloChat.modeAnywhereDesc: Two devices anywhere in the world. Messages travel through a cloud relay — real internet routing.
helloChat.roomCode: Room code (leave empty to create a new room)
helloChat.emptyChat: Send a message to see it travel through the network stack.
helloChat.openTab: Open another tab at this URL to chat with yourself.
helloChat.shareUrlNetwork: Share this URL with someone on your network to chat peer-to-peer.
helloChat.shareUrlAnywhere: Share this URL with anyone to start chatting.
helloChat.noPacket: Send or receive a message to see the protocol layers.
helloChat.syntheticField: This field is simulated — in this mode, the value is generated to show you what it would look like on a real network.
helloChat.endiannessExplain: Your CPU (x86) stores this number as least-significant byte first (little-endian). But the network standard (RFC 1700) requires most-significant byte first (big-endian). This is called "network byte order."
helloChat.endiannessC: In C, you convert with: htons() for 16-bit, htonl() for 32-bit (host-to-network). And ntohs()/ntohl() for the reverse.
helloChat.bitstreamLabel: These bits would travel as electrical pulses through a cable or radio waves through WiFi.
helloChat.reqNone: No setup required — open two tabs and start chatting.
helloChat.reqWebRTC: WebRTC-capable browser. Brief cloud signaling, then direct P2P.
helloChat.relayTemporarilyUnavailable: The relay server is temporarily unavailable. You can still use "Same Machine" mode, or self-host the relay.
helloChat.joinSubtitle: You've been invited to a chat room. Enter your name to join.
